subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Heating for a spa pool that shares a water recirculation system with a swimming pool must be by—

ol.li: a heater using reclaimed heat from another process such as reject heat from a refrigeration plant; or
a gas heater that achieves a minimum gross thermal efficiency of 90%; or

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Where some or all of the heating required by (1) is by a gas heater or a heat pump, the spa pool must have—

ol.li: a cover with a minimum R-Value of 0.05; and
a push button and a time switch to control the operation of the heater.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A time switch must be provided to control the operation of a circulation pump for a spa pool having a capacity of 680 L or more.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Where required, a time switch must be capable of switching electric power on and off at variable pre-programmed times and on variable pre-programmed days.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Pipework carrying heated or chilled water for a spa pool must comply with the insulation requirements of J6D9.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A building with gas heated water for spa pool heating must be provided with—

ol.li: reserved electrical capacity sufficient to support future installation of air-source heat pump water heating equipment for the spa pool assuming a nominal coefficient of performance of 3.25; and

reserved space within the main electrical distribution board comprising at least one empty three-phase circuit breaker slot and 4 DIN rail spaces per 35 kW of water heating capacity, labelled to indicate reservation for future installation of water heating equipment for the spa pool; and
reserved space in the electrical riser or supply pathway for future the spa pool heating equipment sized in accordance with (a); and

reserved plant area with—
space to accommodate heating plant of 7 m² plus 0.04 m²/kW of heating capacity; and
sufficient ventilation openings to achieve airflow of 115 L/s per kW of heating capacity, with no recirculation of air; and
a hot water circulation system and associated coils and heat exchangers serving the spa pool, designed to operate a peak temperature of less than 50°C.

subclause.title: QLD REPLACE SubClause
subclause.p: This sub-clause has deliberately been left blank.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The capacity and space required by (6) must be sufficient to serve the same design load used to specify any gas-powered spa pool water heating equipment.

subclause.title: QLD REPLACE SubClause
subclause.p: This sub-clause has deliberately been left blank.
